results.original_prompt: I want you to write me a function in JavaScript that will take an array of numbers as input and then go through each number one by one and check if that number is greater than 10 and if it is then add it to a new array and at the end return that new array with all the numbers that are greater than 10

seer_meta.optimized: Write a JavaScript function that takes an array of numbers, filters for values greater than 10, and returns the filtered array.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundant descriptions of iteration logic ('go through each number one by one', 'add it to a new array', 'at the end return') and replaced with concise 'filters for values greater than 10'.

results.original_prompt: I would like you to help me build an authentication system for my Next.js application. The system should support multiple authentication methods including email and password login, Google OAuth, and GitHub OAuth. When a user signs up with email and password, we need to hash the password using bcrypt before storing it. For OAuth providers, we need to handle the callback URLs properly. After successful authentication, we should generate a JWT token that expires in 7 days and store it in an HTTP-only cookie. The system should also include middleware that checks for the JWT token on protected routes and redirects unauthenticated users to the login page. We also need password reset functionality where users can request a password reset link that gets sent to their email and expires after 1 hour.
results.optimized_prompt: Build Next.js authentication: email/password (bcrypt), Google OAuth, GitHub OAuth. Generate JWT (7d expiry) in HTTP-only cookie. Add middleware for protected routes (redirect to login if unauthenticated). Include password reset via email link (1h expiry).

seer_meta.optimized: Build Next.js authentication: email/password (bcrypt), Google OAuth, GitHub OAuth. Generate JWT (7d expiry) in HTTP-only cookie. Add middleware for protected routes (redirect to login if unauthenticated). Include password reset via email link (1h expiry).
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundancy ('help me', 'we need to', 'should'), converted requirements to imperative list format, eliminated flowery transitions, kept all technical specifics (bcrypt, JWT, HTTP-only, OAuth providers, expiry times).

results.original_prompt: I want to create a real-time chat application using WebSockets. The application should allow users to create chat rooms, join existing rooms, and send messages in real-time. Each message should show the sender's name, timestamp, and the message content. Users should be able to see who is currently online in each room. When a user joins a room, they should see the last 50 messages from the message history. The system should handle disconnections gracefully and notify other users when someone leaves. I also want to add support for typing indicators so users can see when someone else is typing a message. Messages should be stored in a PostgreSQL database for persistence.
results.optimized_prompt: Build real-time chat app with WebSockets supporting: room creation/joining, message persistence (PostgreSQL), sender name + timestamp + content, online user list, last 50 message history on join, graceful disconnection handling with leave notifications, and typing indicators.

seer_meta.optimized: Build real-time chat app with WebSockets supporting: room creation/joining, message persistence (PostgreSQL), sender name + timestamp + content, online user list, last 50 message history on join, graceful disconnection handling with leave notifications, and typing indicators.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundant phrases ('I want to', 'should allow', 'should be able to', 'should handle', 'I also want'), converted scattered requirements into structured feature list, eliminated repetitive 'users' references

results.original_prompt: Please help me set up a CI/CD pipeline for my Node.js project using GitHub Actions. The pipeline should run on every push to main and on pull requests. First, it should run the linting checks using ESLint. Then it should run the unit tests using Jest and generate a coverage report. If the tests pass, it should build the Docker image and tag it with the commit SHA. After building, it should push the image to AWS ECR. Finally, it should deploy the new image to our ECS cluster using a rolling update strategy. The pipeline should also send a Slack notification on failure. For pull requests, it should only run linting and tests without deploying.
results.optimized_prompt: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD for Node.js: (1) Trigger on push to main and PRs (2) Run ESLint, Jest with coverage (3) On main: build Docker image tagged with commit SHA, push to AWS ECR, deploy to ECS with rolling updates (4) Slack notification on failure (5) PRs: lint and test only, skip deployment

seer_meta.optimized: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD for Node.js: (1) Trigger on push to main and PRs (2) Run ESLint, Jest with coverage (3) On main: build Docker image tagged with commit SHA, push to AWS ECR, deploy to ECS with rolling updates (4) Slack notification on failure (5) PRs: lint and test only, skip deployment
seer_meta.explanation: Removed redundancy ('First', 'Then', 'After'), filler phrases ('Please help me', 'should also'), consolidated multi-sentence descriptions into numbered steps, eliminated repetition of 'pipeline' and 'should'

results.original_prompt: My React app is rendering the component twice on every state update. I'm using useEffect with an empty dependency array but it still fires twice. The component fetches data from an API and sometimes shows stale data. I think there might be a race condition. Help me fix this.
results.optimized_prompt: React component renders twice on state updates despite useEffect with empty dependency array. API calls return stale data, suggesting race conditions. Debug and fix.

seer_meta.optimized: React component renders twice on state updates despite useEffect with empty dependency array. API calls return stale data, suggesting race conditions. Debug and fix.
seer_meta.explanation: Removed explanation phrases ('I'm using', 'I think there might be'), redundant context ('every state update' → 'state updates'), and consolidated the problem statement to core issues: double renders, stale data, race conditions.

Tenants should be isolated from each other. Support role-based access control with admin, editor, and viewer roles. Include audit logging for all data changes.

results.optimized_prompt: Explain JavaScript's event loop: call stack, task queue, microtask queue. Show execution order for nested promises and setTimeout with examples.
